%0: Returns the number of parameters received by a multi-line macro. Useful for macros with a variable number of arguments.
%00: Returns the label preceding a macro invocation. If used, the label itself is not emitted by NASM unless explicitly defined.
%abs: Evaluates its argument and returns the absolute decimal value as a single token.
%arg: Simplifies stack-based parameter handling by defining local names for offsets relative to the frame pointer.
%assign: Defines a numeric single-line macro by evaluating an expression at definition time. Can be redefined for use in loops.
%b2hs: Converts a quoted string to a packed hexadecimal representation, optionally using a separator.
%chr: Creates a quoted string from a list of integer byte values (mod 256).
%clear: Clears all user-defined macros or directives of a specific type (e.g., %define, %macro) to reset the environment.
%cond: Expands to the second argument if the first evaluates to true (non-zero), otherwise expands to the third argument.
%count: Expands to the number of arguments passed to a macro.
%defalias: Defines a symbolic link to another macro, allowing renaming while retaining legacy compatibility.
%define: Defines a single-line macro, optionally with parameters, similar to #define in C.
%defstr: Defines a single-line macro and converts the expanded right-hand side into a quoted string.
%deftok: Defines a single-line macro and converts the string form of the second parameter into a sequence of tokens.
%depend: Adds a filename to the dependency list for generated makefiles when using -M options.
%else: Starts an alternative conditional assembly block if the preceding %IF condition was false.
%endif: Ends a conditional assembly block started by %IF.
%endmacro: Ends the definition of a multi-line macro.
%error: Reports a user-defined error during preprocessing and can be configured to terminate assembly.
%eval: Evaluates a numeric expression and expands to the resulting integer constant.
%exitmacro: Immediately terminates the expansion of the current multi-line macro.
%fatal: Reports a fatal error and immediately terminates the assembly process.
%find: Returns the 1-based index of the first matching string in a list, or 0 if no match is found (case-sensitive).
%findi: Returns the 1-based index of the first matching string in a list, or 0 if no match is found (case-insensitive).
%hex: Evaluates an expression and expands to its unsigned hexadecimal result with a '0x' prefix.
%hs2b: Converts a hexadecimal string into a quoted string of bytes. Pairs of hex digits encode each byte.
%if: Starts a conditional assembly block based on whether a numeric expression evaluates to true.
%ifctx: Assembles subsequent code only if the top of the context stack matches one of the provided names.
%ifdef: Assembles code if a single-line macro or preprocessor function is defined.
%ifdefalias: Assembles code if a single-line macro alias is defined.
%ifdirective: Assembles code if the argument is a supported preprocessor directive, assembler directive, or pseudo-instruction.
%ifempty: Assembles code if the expanded parameters contain no tokens other than whitespace.
%ifenv: Assembles code if the specified environment variable exists.
%iffile: Assembles code if the specified file exists and is readable by NASM.
%ifid: Assembles code if the first token in the parameter is an identifier. Does not include $ or $$.
%ifidn: Assembles code if two pieces of text are identical after macro expansion (case-sensitive).
%ifidni: Assembles code if two pieces of text are identical after macro expansion (case-insensitive).
%ifmacro: Assembles code if defining a macro with the given name and parameters would cause a conflict.
%ifnum: Assembles code if the first token in the parameter is a numeric constant.
%ifstr: Assembles code if the first token in the parameter is a quoted string.
%iftoken: Assembles code if the expanded parameters consist of exactly one token.
%ifusable: Assembles code if the argument is a valid name for a standard macro package available in NASM.
%ifusing: Assembles code if the specified standard macro package is currently in use.
%include: Includes the contents of another source file into the current file at the point of the directive.
%is: Used within %if to check the type of a macro parameter or token (e.g., %if %1 %is id).
%isid: Used within %if to check if a token is a valid identifier.
%line: Notifies NASM that the current line corresponds to a specific line number in an original source file.
%local: Defines local labels within a macro context, automatically prefixed with a unique identifier.
%macro: Begins the definition of a multi-line macro with a name and optional parameter range.
%map: Iterates a macro over a list of arguments, expanding to a comma-separated list of the results.
%note: Adds a message to the listing file without outputting to the console or error file.
%null: Ignores its arguments and expands to nothing.
%num: Converts a numeric expression into a quoted string with optional formatting for base and digit count.
%ord: Expands to a comma-separated list of integer byte values from a quoted string.
%pathsearch: Resolves a filename using the include path and defines a macro with the full path if found.
%pop: Removes the top context from the preprocessor's context stack.
%pragma: Controls various internal NASM options and limits, organized by namespaces like preproc or limit.
%push: Pushes a new context onto the preprocessor's context stack for nested macro management.
%realpath: Expands to the absolute path of a file if supported by the host operating system.
%rep: Replicates a block of code a specified number of times at the preprocessor level.
%repl: Replaces the name of the top context on the stack without affecting its local labels or macros.
%rotate: Rotates the list of macro parameters by a specified number of places (left for positive, right for negative).
%sel: Selects and expands an argument from a list based on a 1-based index.
%selbits: Selects an argument based on whether the current assembly mode is 16, 32, or 64 bits.
%stacksize: Sets the default stack frame size and addressing mode for %arg and %local directives.
%str: Converts its argument into a quoted string, preserving commas and whitespace.
%strcat: Concatenates multiple quoted strings into a single-line macro or expands to the result.
%strlen: Calculates the length of a string and assigns it to a macro as a numeric value.
%substr: Extracts a substring from a string macro starting at a given position for an optional length.
%tok: Converts a quoted string into a sequence of tokens for further preprocessor expansion.
%undef: Removes the definition of a single-line macro.
%unimacro: Removes a case-insensitive multi-line macro matching the specified name and parameters.
%unmacro: Removes a case-sensitive multi-line macro matching the specified name and parameters.
%use: Includes a named standard macro package (e.g., altreg, smartalign) into the assembly.
%warning: Issues a user-defined warning message during preprocessing without stopping assembly.
%xdefine: Defines a single-line macro where embedded macros are expanded at the time of definition rather than expansion.
30: Sub-state within a C-state (Intel processors only).
313: Reserved; must be set to zero.
64bit: x87 Non-Waiting instructions added in various CPU architectures.
6d: INSW: Input string from port to memory.
6f: OUTSW: Output string from memory to port.
74: Target CPU power C-state during wait, minus 1.
9c: PUSHF: Pushes the FLAGS register onto the stack.
9d: POPF: Pops a value from the stack into the FLAGS register.
a5: MOVSW: Moves a word from DS:[SI] to ES:[DI].
a7: CMPSW: Compares words at DS:[SI] and ES:[DI] and sets flags.
aaa: ASCII Adjust After Addition. Adjusts the AL register after BCD addition.
aad: ASCII Adjust Before Division. Converts BCD value in AH:AL to binary in AL.
aam: ASCII Adjust After Multiply. Converts binary value in AL to BCD in AH:AL.
aas: ASCII Adjust AL After Subtraction. Affects AF and CF flags.
ab: STOSW: Stores the word in AX into ES:[DI].
ac: Undefined instructions reserved for generating #UD (invalid opcode) exceptions.
adc: Add with Carry: Adds source, destination, and CF. Stores result in destination.
adcx: Add with Carry: Adds source to destination and CF, affecting only the CF flag.
add: Adds source to destination and stores the result in destination. Affects OF, SF, ZF, AF, CF, and PF.
af: SCASW: Compares AX with word at ES:[DI] and sets flags.
ah: 8-bit high half of the AX register.
al: 8-bit low half of the AX register.
and: Performs bitwise AND between operands. Affects SF, ZF, PF, and clears OF/CF.
arpl: Adjust Requested Privilege Level of a selector to ensure it is not more privileged than a reference.
bh: 8-bit high half of the BX register.
bl: 8-bit low half of the BX register.
bound: Checks if a register value is within memory-specified signed bounds; raises #BR if out of range.
bsf: Bit Scan Forward: Finds the index of the least significant set bit. Sets ZF if no bit is set.
bsr: Bit Scan Reverse: Finds the index of the most significant set bit. Sets ZF if no bit is set.
bswap: Reverses the byte order of a 32-bit or 64-bit register to convert between big-endian and little-endian.
bt: Bit Test: Copies the specified bit of the first operand into the Carry Flag (CF).
btc: Bit Test and Complement: Copies the specified bit to CF and then toggles it in the destination.
btr: Bit Test and Reset: Copies the specified bit to CF and then clears it in the destination.
bts: Bit Test and Set: Copies the specified bit to CF and then sets it in the destination.
bzhi: Zeroes out bits from a specified position up to the MSB in the source and stores result in destination.
call: Pushes the return address onto the stack and jumps to the target procedure.
cbw: Converts signed byte in AL to signed word in AX via sign extension.
cdqe: Sign-extends EAX into RAX (64-bit mode only).
cf: IRET: Interrupt Return. Pops IP, CS, and FLAGS (or EIP, CS, EFLAGS) from stack.
ch: 8-bit high half of the CX register.
cl: 8-bit low half of the CX register.
cl1invmb: Intel SCC-specific instruction to invalidate all message buffers.
clac: Clear Alignment Check flag in EFLAGS (Supervisor Mode Access Prevention).
cld: Clears the Direction Flag (DF = 0), making string instructions increment indices.
cldemote: Hints the CPU to move a cache line to a more distant level of the hierarchy.
clflush: Flushes a cache line from all levels of the cache hierarchy to memory.
clflushopt: Optimized cache line flush with more relaxed ordering for better performance.
cli: Clears the Interrupt Flag (IF = 0), disabling maskable hardware interrupts.
clts: Clears the Task-Switched (TS) flag in Control Register CR0.
clwb: Writes back a modified cache line to memory without necessarily invalidating it.
clzero: Zeroes an entire cache line at the specified memory address (AMD-specific).
cmc: Complements the Carry Flag (CF = NOT CF).
cmovcc: Conditional Move: Copies source to destination if the specified condition is met.
cmp: Subtracts source from destination and sets flags (OF, SF, ZF, AF, CF, PF) without storing the result.
cmpccxadd: Atomically compares memory to a register and adds another register value if the condition is met.
cmpsb: Compares bytes at DS:[SI] and ES:[DI] and updates flags. Increments/decrements SI/DI based on DF.
cmpsq: Compares quadwords at DS:[RSI] and ES:[RDI] and updates flags.
cmpsw: Compares words at DS:[SI] and ES:[DI] and updates flags.
cmpxchg: Compares AL/AX/EAX/RAX with destination; if equal, destination = source. Sets ZF.
cmpxchg16b: Atomic compare and exchange of 16 bytes. Requires a LOCK prefix for atomicity.
cmpxchg8b: Atomic compare and exchange of 8 bytes. Compares EDX:EAX with memory.
condition: x87 floating-point conditional instructions introduced in Pentium Pro.
conditional: x87 floating-point conditional move and compare instructions.
control: x87 basic arithmetic and control instructions with integer arguments.
cpuid: Returns processor identification and feature information based on the value in EAX/ECX.
cqo: Sign-extends RAX into RDX:RAX (64-bit mode only).
cr0: Control Register 0: Controls operating mode and states of the processor.
cr1: Control Register 1: Reserved for future use.
cr2: Control Register 2: Contains the Page Fault Linear Address (PFLA).
cr3: Control Register 3: Contains the physical address of the page directory/PML4 table.
cr4: Control Register 4: Controls various architectural extensions and features.
cr8: Control Register 8: Task Priority Register (TPR) in 64-bit mode.
crc32: Calculates the CRC-32C checksum of the source and accumulates it in the destination.
crx: Moves data between a control register and a general-purpose register.
d8: x87 FPU opcode escape for various floating-point instructions.
d9: x87 FPU opcode escape for various floating-point instructions.
da: x87 FPU opcode escape for conditional move instructions.
daa: Decimal Adjust AL After Addition. Adjusts AL for BCD addition.
das: Decimal Adjust AL After Subtraction. Adjusts AL for BCD subtraction. Affects CF, AF, ZF, SF, and PF.
db: x87 FPU opcode escape for conditional move instructions.
dc: x87 FPU opcode escape for various floating-point instructions.
dd: x87 FPU opcode escape for various floating-point instructions.
de: x87 FPU opcode escape for various floating-point instructions.
dec: Decrements the operand by 1. Affects OF, SF, ZF, AF, and PF (CF is NOT affected).
df: x87 FPU opcode escape for various floating-point instructions.
dh: 8-bit high half of the DX register.
div: Unsigned Divide: Divides accumulator by source. Result in AL/AX/EAX/RAX, remainder in AH/DX/EDX/RDX.
dl: 8-bit low half of the DX register.
dr4: Debug Register 4: Alias for DR6 if DE=1, else reserved.
dr5: Debug Register 5: Alias for DR7 if DE=1, else reserved.
drx: Moves data between a debug register and a general-purpose register.
e3cbf: JCXZ: Jump if CX is zero.
encls: Executes an Intel SGX supervisor function specified in EAX.
endbr32: Indirect Branch Tracking hint to terminate a 32-bit indirect branch.
enqcmd: Atomically enqueues a 64-byte command to a device using a user-mode address.
enter: Creates a stack frame for a procedure with a specified size and nesting level.
erets: Returns from an Intel FRED (Flexible Return and Event Delivery) event handler.
esc: Escape prefix used for floating-point instructions (8087).
f2xm1: Calculates 2^st(0) - 1 and stores the result in st(0).
fabs: Calculates the absolute value of the floating-point value in st(0).
fadd: Adds floating-point source to destination and stores the result in destination.
faddp: Adds st(0) to st(i), stores result in st(i), and pops st(0).
fbld: Converts 10-byte BCD value in memory to floating-point and pushes onto the FPU stack.
fbstp: Converts floating-point value in st(0) to 10-byte BCD, stores in memory, and pops st(0).
fchs: Complements the sign bit of the floating-point value in st(0).
fcmovb: Conditional move to st(0) if the Carry Flag (CF) is set (st(i) < st(0)).
fcom: Compares st(0) with source and updates FPU status flags C0, C2, and C3.
fcomi: Compares st(0) with st(i) and updates CPU flags ZF, PF, and CF directly.
fcomip: Compares st(0) with st(i), updates CPU flags ZF, PF, and CF, and pops st(0).
fcomp: Compares st(0) with source, updates FPU status flags, and pops st(0).
fcompp: Compares st(0) with st(1), updates FPU status flags, and pops both st(0) and st(1).
fcos: Calculates the cosine of the value in st(0) and stores the result in st(0).
fdecstp: Decrements the FPU stack pointer in the status word.
fdisi8087nop: 8087 FPU disable interrupts; acts as NOP on later processors.
fdiv: Divides floating-point destination by source and stores result in destination.
fdivp: Divides st(i) by st(0), stores result in st(i), and pops st(0).
fdivr: Reverse Divide: Divides floating-point source by destination and stores result in destination.
fdivrp: Reverse divides st(i) by st(0), stores result in st(i), and pops st(0).
feni8087nop: 8087 FPU enable interrupts; acts as NOP on later processors.
ffree: Marks the specified FPU register as empty.
ffreep: Marks st(i) as empty and pops the FPU stack.
fiadd: Adds an integer source to st(0) and stores the result in st(0).
ficom: Compares st(0) with an integer source.
ficomp: Compares st(0) with an integer source and pops the FPU stack.
fidiv: Divides st(0) by an integer source.
fidivr: Reverse divides st(0) by an integer source (integer / st(0)).
fild: Converts an integer source in memory to floating-point and pushes it onto the FPU stack.
fimul: Multiplies st(0) by an integer source.
fincstp: Increments the FPU stack pointer in the status word.
fist: Converts value in st(0) to integer and stores it in memory.
fistp: Converts value in st(0) to integer, stores it in memory, and pops the FPU stack.
fisttp: Converts value in st(0) to integer using truncation and pops the FPU stack.
fisub: Subtracts an integer source from st(0).
fisubr: Reverse subtracts an integer source from st(0) (integer - st(0)).
fld: Pushes a floating-point value onto the FPU stack.
fld1: Pushes the constant +1.0 onto the FPU stack.
fldcw: Loads the FPU control word from memory.
fldenv: Loads the FPU environment state from memory.
fldl2e: Pushes log2(e) onto the FPU stack.
fldl2t: Pushes log2(10) onto the FPU stack.
fldlg2: Pushes log10(2) onto the FPU stack.
fldln2: Pushes ln(2) onto the FPU stack.
fldpi: Pushes the constant Pi onto the FPU stack.
fldz: Pushes the constant +0.0 onto the FPU stack.
fmulp: Multiplies st(i) and st(0), stores result in st(i), and pops st(0).
fnclex: Clears all FPU exception flags without checking for pending exceptions.
fninit: Initializes the FPU to its default state without checking for pending exceptions.
fnsave: Saves the FPU state to memory without checking for pending exceptions.
fnsetpm: Sets the FPU to protected mode (80287 only).
fnstcw: Stores the FPU control word to memory without checking for pending exceptions.
fnstenv: Stores the FPU environment to memory without checking for pending exceptions.
fnstsw: Stores the FPU status word to AX or memory without checking for pending exceptions.
fpatan: Calculates the arctangent of st(1)/st(0), stores the result in st(1), and pops st(0).
fprem: Calculates the partial remainder of st(0)/st(1).
fprem1: Calculates the IEEE-compliant partial remainder of st(0)/st(1).
fptan: Calculates the tangent of st(0), pushes result and then 1.0 onto the FPU stack.
frndint: Rounds st(0) to an integer based on the current FPU rounding mode.
frstor: Restores the FPU state from memory.
fscale: Scales st(0) by a power of 2 using the value in st(1).
fsetpm: Sets the FPU to protected mode (80287 only).
fsetpm287nop: 80287 FPU set protected mode; acts as NOP on later processors.
fsin: Calculates the sine of st(0) and stores the result in st(0).
fsincos: Calculates both sine and cosine of st(0), result in st(0) and st(1).
fsqrt: Calculates the square root of st(0).
fst: Stores the value in st(0) into a memory location or another FPU register.
fstp: Stores the value in st(0) and pops the FPU stack.
fstpnce: Floating-point store and pop without checking for exceptions.
fsubp: Subtracts st(0) from st(i), result in st(i), and pops st(0).
fsubr: Reverse Subtract: Subtracts destination from source.
fsubrp: Reverse subtracts st(i) from st(0), result in st(i), and pops st(0).
ftst: Compares st(0) with 0.0 and updates FPU status flags.
fucom: Unordered compare st(0) with source; does not raise an exception for NaNs.
fucomi: Unordered compare st(0) with st(i) and updates CPU flags directly.
fucomip: Unordered compare st(0) with st(i), updates CPU flags, and pops st(0).
fucomp: Unordered compare st(0) with source and pops the FPU stack.
fucompp: Unordered compare st(0) with st(1) and pops both registers.
fwait: Synchronizes CPU and FPU; waits for pending FPU exceptions to be handled.
fxam: Examines the value in st(0) and reports its type in the FPU status word.
fxch: Exchanges the values in st(0) and another FPU register.
fxrstor: Restores FPU, MMX, and SSE state from memory.
fxsave: Saves FPU, MMX, and SSE state to memory.
fxtract: Extracts the exponent and mantissa of st(0) into two separate FPU registers.
fyl2x: Calculates st(1) * log2(st(0)) and pops the stack.
fyl2xp1: Calculates st(1) * log2(st(0) + 1) and pops the stack.
getsec: Executes an Intel SMX (Safer Mode Extensions) leaf function specified in EAX.
hintnottaken: Branch hint prefix indicating the jump is unlikely to be taken.
hlt: Stops instruction execution and places the CPU in a halt state until an interrupt occurs.
hreset: Resets hardware prediction history components specified by a bitmap in EAX.
hwnt: Branch hint prefix indicating the jump is unlikely to be taken.
ibhf: Indirect Branch History Fence to mitigate branch history-based attacks.
ibts: Bitfield Insert: Inserts a bitfield from a register into a memory location (early 386).
idiv: Signed Divide: Divides accumulator by source. Result in AL/AX/EAX/RAX, remainder in AH/DX/EDX/RDX.
iimul: Signed integer multiply with 3 operands and an immediate value.
imme: Signed integer multiply with 3 operands and an immediate value.
imul: Signed Integer Multiply: Multiplies operands and stores result in destination. Affects OF and CF.
in: Input from Port: Copies data from an I/O port into the accumulator (AL/AX/EAX).
inc: Increments the operand by 1. Affects OF, SF, ZF, AF, and PF (CF is NOT affected).
incsspd: Increments the shadow stack pointer by the value in the destination register.
insb: Inputs a byte from port DX to memory at ES:[DI]. Increments/decrements DI based on DF.
insw: Inputs a word from port DX to memory at ES:[DI].
int: Calls a software interrupt by looking up the interrupt vector in the Interrupt Vector Table (IVT) or Interrupt Descriptor Table (IDT).
int1: Triggers a single-step or debug exception (interrupt 1).
into: Triggers interrupt 4 if the Overflow Flag (OF) is set.
invd: Invalidates internal caches without writing back modified data to memory.
invlpg: Invalidates the TLB entry for the specified memory address.
invlpgb: Invalidates TLB entries for a range of pages across all processors (AMD-specific).
invpcid: Invalidates TLB and paging-structure caches based on a specified PCID.
iret: Interrupt Return: Pops IP, CS, and FLAGS from stack to return from an interrupt handler.
iretq: 64-bit Return from Interrupt. Pops RIP, CS, RFLAGS, RSP, and SS from stack.
jcc: Conditional Jumps (e.g., JE, JNE, JZ, JNZ). Transfers control if the specified condition is met.
jccrel16: Near conditional jump with a 16-bit relative offset.
jccrel32: Near conditional jump with a 32-bit relative offset.
jcxz: Jump if CX register is zero.
jmp: Unconditionally transfers program control to the target address.
jmpai: Jump to and execute instructions in the Alternate Instruction Set (VIA/Centaur).
jrcxz: Jump if RCX register is zero (64-bit mode).
lahf: Loads the status flags (SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF) into the AH register.
lar: Loads access rights from a segment descriptor into a register and sets ZF on success.
lds: Loads a far pointer from memory into DS and a destination register.
lea: Load Effective Address: Calculates the address of the source operand and stores it in the destination.
leave: Releases the stack frame created by ENTER by restoring RSP and RBP.
les: Loads a far pointer from memory into ES and a destination register.
lfence: Serializes load operations, ensuring all preceding loads complete before subsequent loads.
lfs: Loads a far pointer from memory into FS and a destination register.
lgdt: Loads the Global Descriptor Table Register (GDTR) from memory.
lidt: Loads the Interrupt Descriptor Table Register (IDTR) from memory.
lldt: Loads the Local Descriptor Table Register (LDTR) from a register or memory.
lmsw: Loads the Machine Status Word (lower 16 bits of CR0).
loadall: Undocumented instruction to load all CPU registers from a fixed memory location (80286).
loadall386t: Loads all 386 registers, including hidden segment descriptor parts, from memory.
loadalld: Loads all registers and hidden segment state from memory (80386).
loadalll: Loads all registers and hidden segment state from memory (80286).
lock: Prefix that ensures atomic execution of the following instruction in multi-processor systems.
lodsb: Loads the byte at DS:[SI] into AL and increments/decrements SI based on DF.
lodsd: Loads the doubleword at DS:[SI] into EAX and increments/decrements SI.
lodsq: Loads the quadword at DS:[RSI] into RAX and increments/decrements RSI.
lodsw: Loads the word at DS:[SI] into AX and increments/decrements SI.
loop: Decrements CX and jumps if CX is not zero. Variants like LOOPE check ZF as well.
loopx: Family of loop instructions that decrement a counter register and conditionally jump.
lsl: Loads the segment limit from a segment descriptor and sets ZF on success.
ltrr: Loads the Task Register (TR) from a register or memory to specify the current TSS.
m128: 128-bit memory operand descriptor for instructions like INVPCID.
m1616: 16-bit array bound descriptor for the BOUND instruction.
m224e: Memory operand for FSTENV/FLDENV in 32-bit protected mode.
m3216: Far pointer memory operand consisting of a 32-bit offset and 16-bit segment.
m512: 512-bit (64-byte) memory operand for instructions like ENQCMD and MOVDIR64B.
m864e: Memory operand for FSAVE/FRSTOR in 32-bit protected mode.
mcommit: Ensures all preceding stores have been committed to memory and checks for errors (AMD-specific).
mm: Undocumented MMX-specific instructions for byte swapping.
mnemonic: Placeholder for various x87 FPU control mnemonics.
mnemonicb: Placeholder for various x87 FPU control mnemonics with non-waiting variants.
monitor: Sets up a linear address range for the CPU to monitor for writes, used with MWAIT.
monitorl: Sets up a linear address range for monitoring (32-bit/64-bit variant).
monitorx: User-mode variant of the MONITOR instruction (AMD-specific).
mov: Copies the value from the source operand to the destination. Does not affect flags.
movbe: Moves data between memory and register with byte-order reversal (Big-Endian to/from Little-Endian).
movdb: Undocumented Cyrix-specific move data between debug and general registers.
movdir64b: Atomically moves 64 bytes of data from memory to a destination using a direct store.
movdiri: Moves data from a register to memory using a direct store that bypasses the cache.
movsb: Moves a byte from DS:[SI] to ES:[DI]. Increments/decrements SI and DI based on DF.
movsd: Moves a doubleword from DS:[SI] to ES:[DI].
movsq: Moves a quadword from DS:[RSI] to ES:[RDI].
movsw: Moves a word from DS:[SI] to ES:[DI].
movsx: Moves a source value to a larger destination register with sign-extension.
movsxd: Moves a 32-bit source value to a 64-bit destination register with sign-extension.
movzx: Moves a source value to a larger destination register with zero-extension.
mul: Unsigned Multiply: Multiplies accumulator by source. Result in AX/DX:AX/EDX:EAX/RDX:RAX. Affects OF and CF.
neg: Two's Complement Negation: Subtracts the operand from 0 and stores the result back. Affects CF, ZF, SF, OF, AF, PF.
no: Reserved opcodes, often used by specific CPU vendors for internal testing.
nop: No Operation: Performs no action and does not affect flags or registers, except for the instruction pointer.
nopl: Long NOP: A multi-byte NOP used for code alignment without affecting execution state.
not: One's Complement Negation: Inverts all bits of the operand. Does not affect flags.
oio: Undocumented I/O instructions for specific CPU architectures.
or: Performs bitwise OR between operands. Affects SF, ZF, PF, and clears OF/CF.
out: Output to Port: Copies data from the accumulator (AL/AX/EAX) to an I/O port.
outsb: Outputs a byte from memory at DS:[SI] to port DX. Increments/decrements SI based on DF.
outsw: Outputs a word from memory at DS:[SI] to port DX.
patch2: Microarchitecture-specific patch or data read instruction (Intel/AMD).
pbndkb: Platform-specific instruction to bind data to a platform using encryption.
pconfig: Configures platform features (e.g., Intel MKTME) based on a function code in EAX.
pna: Branch hint prefix indicating the jump is unlikely to be taken.
pop: Loads the value from the top of the stack into the destination and increments the stack pointer.
popa: Pops all 16-bit general-purpose registers (DI, SI, BP, SP, BX, DX, CX, AX) from the stack.
popcnt: Counts the number of bits set to 1 in the source operand and stores result in destination.
popf: Pops a 16-bit or 32-bit value from the stack into the FLAGS/EFLAGS register.
popfq: Pops a 64-bit value from the stack into the RFLAGS register.
precision: Controls FPU precision (part of FPU control instructions).
prefetchit0: Prefetches an instruction cache line to all levels of the cache hierarchy.
prefetchnta: Prefetches data into the cache while indicating it is non-temporal (likely used only once).
prefetchw: Prefetches a cache line into the cache and marks it as modified (intent-to-write).
prefetchwt1: Prefetches a cache line into the L2 cache with intent-to-write hint.
pswapw: MMX instruction that swaps 16-bit words within a 64-bit register (AMD-specific).
ptwrite: Writes data into the Intel Processor Trace stream for debugging and profiling.
push: Decrements the stack pointer and stores the source operand at the top of the stack.
pusha: Pushes all 16-bit general-purpose registers (AX, CX, DX, BX, SP, BP, SI, DI) onto the stack.
pushf: Pushes the FLAGS/EFLAGS register onto the stack.
pushfq: Pushes the RFLAGS register onto the stack.
range: Indicates x87 transcendental instructions with specific input ranges.
rb: Zeroes high bits from a specified position; part of the BZHI instruction logic.
rcl: Rotate Left through Carry: Rotates bits left, including the Carry Flag in the rotation loop.
rcr: Rotate Right through Carry: Rotates bits right, including the Carry Flag in the rotation loop.
rdfsbase: Reads the base address of the FS segment into a destination register.
rdmsr: Read Model Specific Register: Reads the 64-bit MSR specified in ECX into EDX:EAX.
rdmsrlist: Reads a list of MSRs into memory based on a bitmap (Intel-specific).
rdpid: Reads the processor core ID into the specified destination register.
rdpkru: Reads the Protection Key Rights for User Pages register (PKRU) into EAX.
rdpmc: Read Performance Monitoring Counter: Reads the counter specified in ECX into EDX:EAX.
rdpru: Reads selected supervisor-level registers (e.g., performance counters) in user mode (AMD).
rdtsc: Read Time Stamp Counter: Reads the 64-bit cycle counter into EDX:EAX.
rdtscp: Read Time Stamp Counter and Processor ID: Reads the TSC into EDX:EAX and Core ID into ECX.
reg: Generic placeholder for instructions moving data between registers and control/debug registers.
rep: Repeat prefix for string instructions; repeats the operation while CX > 0.
repnz: Repeat Not Zero prefix; repeats string operations while CX > 0 and ZF = 0.
repxx: Group of repeat prefixes (REP, REPE, REPNE, REPNZ, REPZ).
ret: Pops the return address from the stack and transfers control back to it.
retf: Far Return: Pops both the instruction pointer and code segment from the stack.
retn: Near Return: Pops the instruction pointer from the stack within the same code segment.
rexwprefix: Instruction prefix used in 64-bit mode to specify 64-bit operand size.
rol: Rotate Left: Rotates bits of the operand to the left. Bits rotated out the MSB side wrap to the LSB.
ror: Rotate Right: Rotates bits of the operand to the right. Bits rotated out the LSB side wrap to the MSB.
rsm68: Resume from System Management Mode: Restores CPU state from the SMM save area.
sahf: Stores bits from the AH register into the status flags (SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF).
sal: Shift Arithmetic Left: Performs bitwise left shift, effectively multiplying by powers of 2.
salc: Set AL on Carry: Sets AL to 0xFF if CF=1, or 0x00 if CF=0 (undocumented).
sar: Shift Arithmetic Right: Performs signed bitwise right shift, preserving the sign bit.
saveall: Undocumented instruction that saves all CPU state; may cause system hang if misused.
sbb: Integer Subtraction with Borrow: destination = (destination - (source + CF)). Affects CF, ZF, SF, OF, AF, PF.
scasb: Compares AL with byte at ES:[DI] and updates flags. Increments/decrements DI based on DF.
scasq: Compares RAX with quadword at ES:[RDI] and updates flags.
scasw: Compares AX with word at ES:[DI] and updates flags.
senduipi: Sends an inter-processor user interrupt (Intel User Interrupts).
serialize: Serializes all preceding and subsequent instructions to ensure execution order and state.
setalc: Undocumented alias for SALC: Set AL on Carry flag.
setcc: Sets the destination byte to 1 if the specified condition is met, otherwise sets it to 0.
sgdt: Stores the Global Descriptor Table Register (GDTR) into the specified memory location.
shl: Shift Logical Left: Performs bitwise left shift, filling the LSB with 0. Affects CF, ZF, SF.
shld: Double Precision Shift Left: Shifts bits from source into destination while shifting destination left.
shr: Shift Logical Right: Performs bitwise right shift, filling the MSB with 0. Affects CF, ZF, SF.
shrd: Double Precision Shift Right: Shifts bits from source into destination while shifting destination right.
sidt: Stores the Interrupt Descriptor Table Register (IDTR) into memory.
sldt: Stores the Local Descriptor Table Register (LDTR) into a register or memory.
smsw: Stores the Machine Status Word (lower 16 bits of CR0) into a register or memory.
source: Placeholder for FPU source operand descriptions.
std: Sets the Direction Flag (DF = 1), causing string instructions to decrement indices.
sti: Sets the Interrupt Flag (IF = 1), enabling maskable hardware interrupts.
storeall: Undocumented instruction; purpose unknown, potentially related to ICE mode.
storealll: Undocumented instruction that stores all CPU registers to memory and halts the CPU.
stosb: Stores the byte in AL into ES:[DI] and increments/decrements DI based on DF.
stosd: Stores the doubleword in EAX into ES:[DI].
stosq: Stores the quadword in RAX into ES:[RDI].
stosw: Stores the word in AX into ES:[DI].
str: Stores the Task Register (TR) into a register or memory.
sub: Subtracts source from destination and stores the result in destination. Affects OF, SF, ZF, AF, CF, and PF.
swapgs: Exchanges the current GS segment base address with the KernelGSBase MSR value.
syscall: Fast System Call: Switches to ring 0 and transfers control to a pre-defined OS entry point.
sysenter: Fast System Call: Optimized entry into ring 0 for system services.
sysexit: Fast Return from System Call: Optimized return to ring 3 from a SYSENTER call.
sysret: Fast Return from System Call: Optimized return to ring 3 from a SYSCALL call.
test: Performs a bitwise AND between operands and sets flags (ZF, SF, PF) without storing the result.
trx: Moves data between a test register and a general-purpose register (80486 only).
tzcnt: Count Trailing Zeroes: Counts the number of zero bits starting from the LSB. Sets CF if result is 0.
ud0: Undocumented intentionally undefined instruction that triggers a #UD exception.
ud1: Intentionally undefined instruction; triggers a #UD exception.
ud2: Intentionally undefined instruction; triggers a #UD exception, reserved for software testing.
ud2aad: Undefined instruction variant based on AAD opcode.
udb: Undefined instruction that triggers a #UD exception in 64-bit mode.
umonitor: Sets up a memory address for monitoring by the UMWAIT instruction.
umov: User Move: Moves data while ignoring certain privilege checks (ICE mode).
unknown: Placeholder for undocumented or unverified opcodes and instructions.
verr: Verifies that a segment specified by the selector is readable. Sets ZF=1 if valid.
verw: Verifies that a segment specified by the selector is writable. Sets ZF=1 if valid.
wait: Causes the CPU to wait for the FPU to complete its current operation and check for exceptions.
waiting: Group of x87 FPU control instructions that check for pending exceptions.
wbinvd: Writes back all modified cache lines to memory and invalidates all internal caches.
wbnoinvd: Writes back all modified cache lines to memory without invalidating them.
wrmsr: Write Model Specific Register: Writes the 64-bit value in EDX:EAX to the MSR specified in ECX.
wrmsrns: Non-serializing variant of WRMSR for faster MSR updates (Intel-specific).
xacquire: Instruction prefix for Hardware Lock Elision (HLE) to start a transactional execution.
xadd: Exchanges operands and then stores their sum in the destination. Affects CF, ZF, SF, OF, AF, PF.
xbegin: Starts a transactional execution block. If it fails, control jumps to a fallback handler.
xbts: Bitfield Extract: Extracts a bitfield from memory into a register (early 386).
xchg: Atomically exchanges the values of two operands. Does not affect flags.
xlat: Table Look-up Translation: AL = DS:[BX + AL]. Useful for character encoding translation.
xor: Performs bitwise XOR between operands. Often used to zero registers. Affects SF, ZF, PF, and clears OF/CF.
xsave: Saves processor state components specified by a bitmap to a memory area.
xsave64: Saves 64-bit processor state components to a memory area.
xsavec: Saves processor state with compaction to save space in the save area.
xsaveopt: Optimized state save that avoids writing components that haven't changed.
xsaves: Saves supervisor state components with compaction and optimization.
xsaves64: Saves 64-bit supervisor state components with compaction.
xsusldtrk: Suspends or resumes load address tracking for Intel TSX.
zxpause: Pauses the processor until the TSC reaches a specified value; supports low-power C-states (Zhaoxin).
